evals.prompt: Build an LBO model for a $50M EBITDA software company at 12x entry multiple with 5-year hold and 12x exit
evals.expected_output: 6-sheet Excel workbook with complete LBO analysis showing IRR, MoM, and debt paydown
expectations: Creates an .xlsx file with exactly 6 sheets
Sources & Uses balances (total sources = total uses)
Debt schedule shows amortization and cash sweep for each tranche
Returns analysis calculates IRR and MoM correctly
Covenant sheet tracks leverage ratios across all years
Reports IRR, MoM, and leverage at entry and exit

evals.prompt: LBO model but I only know EBITDA is $30M. Use PE industry defaults.
evals.expected_output: Complete LBO model using standard PE assumptions with all defaults documented
expectations: Applies default entry multiple in 8-12x range
Structures debt at reasonable leverage (4-6x EBITDA)
Equity check is 30-40% of purchase price
All defaults documented in Transaction Summary
Returns analysis provides base case IRR

evals.prompt: Create an LBO with 8x leverage, $100M EBITDA, 15x entry multiple, and 10x exit multiple
evals.expected_output: Model built with warnings about high leverage and exit multiple below entry
expectations: Warns that 8x leverage exceeds typical 7x threshold
Flags exit multiple (10x) below entry multiple (15x) as a risk
Covenant sheet shows potential breaches from high leverage
Still builds the model as requested with clear warnings
Sensitivity table shows IRR under various exit scenarios
